records: DIRECT REMATCH OF 2022 GROUP STAGE — MOROCCO ELIMINATED CANADA THEN AND ARE BACK TO DO IT AGAIN: Morocco beat Canada 2-1 in the 2022 World Cup group stage, with goals from Ziyech and En-Nesyri inside 23 minutes. Canada have never beaten Morocco in four all-time meetings (W0 D1 L3). A Canada win today would be their first ever victory over Morocco — in the most important match either nation has played.

CANADA'S FIRST-EVER ROUND OF 16 — EVERY MATCH FROM HERE REWRITES HISTORY: Canada have broken records at every stage of this tournament. First point. First win. First knockout win. First Round of 16. A quarterfinal would be so far beyond anything Canadian football has achieved it would enter the realm of the miraculous.

MOROCCO UNBEATEN IN 33 CONSECUTIVE MATCHES — THE LONGEST ACTIVE UNBEATEN RUN AT THE TOURNAMENT: Morocco's unbeaten run stretches back through the 2025 AFCON title, a perfect qualifying campaign, and now four World Cup matches. Thirty-three matches without defeat is the longest active streak of any nation in international football.

MOROCCO FIRST AFRICAN NATION TO WIN KNOCKOUT MATCHES AT CONSECUTIVE WORLD CUPS: By beating the Netherlands in the Round of 32, Morocco became the first African side ever to win knockout matches at back-to-back World Cups (Spain/Portugal 2022, Netherlands 2026). A win today adds a third consecutive knockout victory — another unprecedented feat for African football.

ISMAEL SAIBARI — 4 GOALS, ONE FROM EQUALLING THE HIGHEST-SCORING AFRICAN IN A SINGLE WORLD CUP: Saibari (3 group stage goals + 1 penalty vs Netherlands) has 4 goals at this tournament. One more draws level with Cameroon's Roger Milla (1990) and Senegal's Ismaila Sarr (2026) as joint-highest scorer by an African player in a single World Cup edition. Two goals breaks it outright.

MOROCCO COMPLETED 801 PASSES AGAINST NETHERLANDS — ONLY THE SECOND TEAM IN WC HISTORY TO EXCEED 800: Morocco's possession game against the Netherlands produced 801 completed passes — the second-highest total ever recorded in a World Cup match since modern data tracking began, behind only Spain. Their 61.75% average possession across the tournament is the highest of any team remaining.

YASSINE BOUNOU WAS BORN IN MONTREAL — CANADA'S NEMESIS IS TECHNICALLY CANADIAN-BORN: Morocco's goalkeeper Bounou was born in Montreal, Canada before his family returned to Morocco when he was young. He faces the Canadian national team today as the last line of Morocco's defence — a Canadian-born goalkeeper potentially ending Canada's dream tournament.

CANADA RECORDED 100 FINAL-THIRD PRESSURES IN ONE HALF AGAINST SOUTH AFRICA — THE HIGHEST RECORDED IN ANY WC MATCH SINCE 2010: Jesse Marsch's intense pressing system produced a record-breaking first-half pressure count against South Africa. Against Morocco's possession-based structure, that pressure output will be Canada's primary weapon — and its greatest risk.

ALPHONSO DAVIES STARTS FOR THE FIRST TIME THIS TOURNAMENT: Davies missed most of the group stage through injury and has only appeared as a substitute. He is expected to make his first start today. Davies is the most recognisable Canadian footballer on earth — a Champions League winner with Bayern Munich. Canada with a fully fit Davies is measurably a different team.

JONATHAN DAVID — 4 WORLD CUP GOALS ALREADY THE MOST BY A CANADIAN IN HISTORY — NEEDS TWO MORE TO REACH A RECORD THAT DIDN'T EXIST BEFORE THIS TOURNAMENT: David's hat-trick against Qatar and goal against Bosnia already made him Canada's all-time leading World Cup scorer. Every goal he scores sets a new Canadian record that was essentially impossible to exist before 2026.

CANADA HAVE MORE SHOTS ON TARGET THAN ANY OTHER TEAM AT THIS WORLD CUP — YET SCORED ONLY 2 GOALS IN THEIR LAST TWO MATCHES: The most dangerous team in terms of shot volume is also the most wasteful in front of goal across the knockout stage. Against Morocco's goalkeeper Bounou — who saved two Netherlands penalties — Canada's finishing will be the difference between history and heartbreak.

MBAPPÉ — 18 WORLD CUP GOALS, TWO BEHIND MESSI'S NEW ALL-TIME RECORD: Mbappé's brace vs Sweden took him to 18 total World Cup goals — one behind Lionel Messi's new record of 20. Two goal today ties Messi. Three goals surpasses the greatest goalscorer in World Cup history. He is 27 years old with at least one more World Cup ahead of him.

MBAPPÉ — MORE WORLD CUP KNOCKOUT GOALS THAN ANY PLAYER IN HISTORY: With 10 knockout stage goals, Mbappé holds the outright all-time record for goals scored in World Cup knockout matches — surpassing Ronaldo, Klose, and Müller. A goal today extends it to 11 in the knockout rounds alone.

MICHAEL OLISE — 5 ASSISTS, ONE FROM EQUALLING PELÉ'S SINGLE-TOURNAMENT RECORD: Olise leads the tournament with 5 assists in 4 matches. Pelé's record of 6 assists in a single World Cup, set in 1958, has stood for 68 years. One more today ties it. Two breaks it outright.

FRANCE FIRST TEAM SINCE BRAZIL 2002 WITH THREE PLAYERS ON 5+ GOAL INVOLVEMENTS: Mbappé (8), Dembélé (6), and Olise (5) have each reached that threshold. The last side to manage it was Brazil's 2002 title-winning team — Ronaldo (8), Rivaldo (6), Ronaldinho (5). France are mirroring the output of arguably the greatest single-tournament attacking performance in World Cup history.

FRANCE COULD BECOME ONLY THE THIRD TEAM TO SCORE 3+ GOALS IN 5 CONSECUTIVE WC MATCHES: France have scored 3+ in all four of their matches at this tournament. Only Germany (1954) and Brazil (1970) have achieved 3+ goals across five consecutive World Cup matches in a single edition — and both those teams won the title.

DESCHAMPS — 18 WC WINS AS MANAGER, THE MOST IN HISTORY. A 19TH TODAY WOULD BE UNPRECEDENTED: Deschamps holds the all-time record for World Cup victories as a head coach. A win vs Paraguay gives him 19 — a number no manager has ever reached in the tournament's history. He has also announced this is his final World Cup before stepping down as France coach.

FRANCE HAVEN'T LOST A ROUND OF 16 MATCH SINCE 1934 — 92 YEARS: France's last elimination in a 16-team knockout round was a 3-2 loss to Austria in 1934 at the inaugural knockout stage. Since the format was reintroduced in 1986, France have won every single Round of 16 match they have played — 7 in a row.

THIS IS A REMATCH OF THE 1998 WORLD CUP ROUND OF 16 — WITH DESCHAMPS NOW IN THE DUGOUT: The last time these nations met at a World Cup was in the Round of 16 at France 1998, which France won 1-0. Didier Deschamps was the captain of that France team. He is now the manager. He has been involved in both World Cup knockout matches France have ever played against Paraguay — 28 years apart.

FRANCE ARE TRYING TO REACH THREE CONSECUTIVE WORLD CUP FINALS — ONLY WEST GERMANY AND BRAZIL HAVE DONE IT: France won in 2018, lost the final in 2022. A run to the final in 2026 would make them only the third nation in history to contest three consecutive World Cup finals, alongside West Germany (1982–1990) and Brazil (1994–2002).

FRANCE UNBEATEN IN 11 WORLD CUP MATCHES AGAINST SOUTH AMERICAN NATIONS SINCE 1978: France's last World Cup loss to a South American side was a 2-1 group stage defeat to Argentina in 1978 — nearly 50 years ago. Since then: W6 D5 against South American opposition at World Cups. Paraguay are South American.

PARAGUAY'S ONLY TWO WORLD CUP KNOCKOUT WINS — BOTH CAME IN PENALTY SHOOTOUTS, BOTH AGAINST NON-EUROPEAN OPPONENTS: Paraguay beat Japan 5-3 on penalties in 2010 after a 0-0 draw. They beat Germany 4-3 on penalties in 2026 after a 1-1 draw. Both times they did not win in 90 minutes. Both times they saved at least two penalties. France have not lost a competitive penalty shootout since Euro 2020.

ORLANDO GILL — FROM UNKNOWN TO NATIONAL HERO IN 120 MINUTES: Paraguay's goalkeeper was barely known outside South America before the Germany match. He saved two penalties in the shootout, was named Man of the Match, and returned home to find Paraguay had declared a national holiday in the team's honour. He now faces Mbappé — arguably the most dangerous penalty taker in world football.
